name: getFlagPlanets
description: Returns whether planets and other solar system bodies are currently displayed.
returnDescription: `true` if planets are visible, `false` if hidden.

name: setFlagPlanets
description: Shows or hides all planets and solar system bodies (Sun, Moon, planets, asteroids, comets).

parameters.name: visible
parameters.description: `true` to show planets, `false` to hide them.

name: getFlagHints
description: Returns whether planet hints (symbol markers around planets) are displayed.

name: setFlagHints
description: Shows or hides planet hints (circle markers around planets). Useful for locating planets when they are small.
example:
// Show planet hints
SolarSystem.setFlagHints(true);

name: getFlagLabels
description: Returns whether planet labels (names) are displayed.

name: setFlagLabels
description: Shows or hides planet labels (names).
example:
// Show planet names
SolarSystem.setFlagLabels(true);

name: getLabelsAmount
description: Returns the amount of planet labels displayed. Higher values show labels for fainter planets.
example:
var labelsAmount = SolarSystem.getLabelsAmount();
core.debug("Planet labels amount: " + labelsAmount);

name: setLabelsAmount
description: Sets the amount of planet labels displayed. `0` = no labels, `10` = maximum labels (shows labels for faintest planets).

parameters.name: amount
parameters.description: Labels amount (0.0 to 10.0). The default value is 3.0.

name: getFlagOrbits
description: Returns whether planet orbits are displayed.

name: setFlagOrbits
description: Shows or hides planet orbits.
example:
// Show planet orbits
SolarSystem.setFlagOrbits(true);

name: getFlagTrails
description: Returns whether planet trails (paths traced over time) are displayed.

name: setFlagTrails
description: Shows or hides planet trails (the path planets have traveled over time). Useful for visualizing planetary motion.
example:
// Show planet motion trails
SolarSystem.setFlagTrails(true);

name: getFlagPointer
description: Returns whether the selection pointer (animated ring) is displayed around selected planets.

name: setFlagPointer
description: Shows or hides the selection pointer (animated ring) around selected planets.
example:
// Enable selection pointer
SolarSystem.setFlagPointer(true);

name: getFlagMarkers
description: Returns whether markers for minor bodies (asteroids, comets) are displayed as small dots.
returnDescription: `true` if minor body markers are visible.

name: setFlagMarkers
description: Shows or hides markers for minor bodies (asteroids, comets, Kuiper belt objects) as small dots.
example:
// Show asteroid and comet markers
SolarSystem.setFlagMarkers(true);

name: getFlagLightTravelTime
description: Returns whether light travel time correction is applied to planet positions (showing where planets actually are vs where they appear due to light speed).
returnDescription: `true` if light travel time is applied.

name: setFlagLightTravelTime
description: Enables or disables light travel time correction for planet positions. When enabled, planets are shown at their light-travel-time corrected positions.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: `true` to enable light travel time correction.

example:
// Enable realistic light travel time
SolarSystem.setFlagLightTravelTime(true);

name: recreateTrails
description: Resets and recreates all planet trails. Useful after changing trail settings or clearing accumulated trail data.

name: reloadPlanets
description: Reloads all solar system data from configuration files. Useful after editing planet data or adding new minor bodies.

name: resetTextures
description: Resets textures for a specific planet, or all planets if the name is empty. Useful after texture updates.

parameters.name: planetName
parameters.description: English name of the planet, or an empty string for all.

name: setTextureForPlanet
description: Replaces the texture of a planet with a custom image. The texture path is relative to the `scripts/` directory.

parameters.name: texturePath
parameters.description: Path to the texture file (placed in the `scripts/` folder).

example:
// Replace Mars texture with custom image
SolarSystem.setTextureForPlanet("Mars", "mars_custom.png");

name: getFlagMoonScale
description: Returns whether the Moon is displayed with an enlarged scale.

name: setFlagMoonScale
description: Enables or disables scaling of the Moon.

parameters.name: enable
parameters.description: `true` to enable Moon scaling, `false` to use real size.

name: getMoonScale
description: Returns the current scaling factor for the Moon. `1.0` = real size, higher values make the Moon appear larger.
example:
var moonScale = SolarSystem.getMoonScale();
core.debug("Moon scale: " + moonScale);

name: setMoonScale
description: Sets the scaling factor for the Moon. Only effective when `flagMoonScale` is `true`.

parameters.name: scale
parameters.description: Scale factor (e.g., 4.0 to make it 4 times larger).

name: getFlagMinorBodyScale
description: Returns whether minor bodies (asteroids, comets, Kuiper belt objects) are displayed with an enlarged scale.
returnDescription: `true` if minor body scaling is enabled.

name: setFlagMinorBodyScale
description: Enables or disables scaling of minor bodies (asteroids, comets, etc.).

name: getMinorBodyScale
description: Returns the current scaling factor for minor bodies (asteroids, comets, etc.).
example:
var minorScale = SolarSystem.getMinorBodyScale();
core.debug("Minor body scale: " + minorScale);

name: setMinorBodyScale
description: Sets the scaling factor for minor bodies. Only effective when `flagMinorBodyScale` is `true`.

parameters.name: scale
parameters.description: Scale factor (e.g., 10.0 to make them 10 times larger).

name: getFlagPlanetScale
description: Returns whether planets (excluding the Sun and Moon) are displayed with an enlarged scale.

name: setFlagPlanetScale
description: Enables or disables scaling of planets (Mercury through Neptune).

name: getPlanetScale
description: Returns the current scaling factor for planets.
example:
var planetScale = SolarSystem.getPlanetScale();
core.debug("Planet scale: " + planetScale);

name: setPlanetScale
description: Sets the scaling factor for planets. Only effective when `flagPlanetScale` is `true`.
example:
SolarSystem.setPlanetScale(200.0);
SolarSystem.setFlagPlanetScale(true);

name: getFlagSunScale
description: Returns whether the Sun is displayed with an enlarged scale.

name: setFlagSunScale
description: Enables or disables scaling of the Sun.

name: getSunScale
description: Returns the current scaling factor for the Sun.
example:
var sunScale = SolarSystem.getSunScale();
core.debug("Sun scale: " + sunScale);

name: setSunScale
description: Sets the scaling factor for the Sun. Only effective when `flagSunScale` is `true`.
example: SolarSystem.setSunScale(5.0);

name: getFlagDrawMoonHalo
description: Returns whether the Moon's halo (glow around the Moon) is drawn when the Moon is near the Sun.

name: setFlagDrawMoonHalo
description: Shows or hides the Moon's halo effect. The halo helps locate the Moon when it is close to the Sun.

parameters.name: draw
parameters.description: `true` to draw Moon halo, `false` to hide.

example:
// Enable Moon halo
SolarSystem.setFlagDrawMoonHalo(true);

name: getFlagDrawSunHalo
description: Returns whether the Sun's halo (glow around the Sun) is drawn.

name: setFlagDrawSunHalo
description: Shows or hides the Sun's halo effect.

parameters.name: draw
parameters.description: `true` to draw Sun halo, `false` to hide.

example: SolarSystem.setFlagDrawSunHalo(true);

name: getFlagIsolatedTrails
description: Returns whether trails are shown only for selected objects rather than all planets.
returnDescription: `true` if only selected objects show trails.

name: setFlagIsolatedTrails
description: Sets whether trails should be shown only for selected objects (isolated) or for all planets.

parameters.name: isolated
parameters.description: `true` to show trails only for selected objects.

example:
// Show trails only for selected planets
SolarSystem.setFlagIsolatedTrails(true);

name: getNumberIsolatedTrails
description: Returns the number of recently selected objects for which isolated trails are shown.
example:
var numTrails = SolarSystem.getNumberIsolatedTrails();
core.debug("Number of isolated trails: " + numTrails);

name: setNumberIsolatedTrails
description: Sets the number of recently selected objects for which isolated trails are shown (1 to 5).
example:
// Show trails for last 3 selected objects
SolarSystem.setNumberIsolatedTrails(3);

name: getFlagIsolatedOrbits
description: Returns whether orbits are shown only for selected objects rather than all planets.
returnDescription: `true` if only selected objects show orbits.

name: setFlagIsolatedOrbits
description: Sets whether orbits should be shown only for selected objects (isolated) or for all planets.

parameters.name: isolated
parameters.description: `true` to show orbits only for selected objects.

example:
// Show orbit only for selected planet
SolarSystem.setFlagIsolatedOrbits(true);

name: getFlagPlanetsOrbits
description: Returns whether orbits of the major planets are displayed, regardless of other orbit settings.
returnDescription: `true` if major planet orbits are visible.

name: setFlagPlanetsOrbits
description: Sets whether orbits of the major planets should be displayed, even when isolated orbits mode is active.
example:
// Show all major planet orbits
SolarSystem.setFlagPlanetsOrbits(true);

name: getFlagPlanetsOrbitsOnly
description: Returns whether only major planets show orbits (excluding minor bodies).
returnDescription: `true` if only major planets show orbits.

name: setFlagPlanetsOrbitsOnly
description: Sets whether only major planets should show orbits (excluding asteroids, comets, etc.).

parameters.name: onlyMajor
parameters.description: `true` to show orbits only for major planets.

example:
// Show orbits only for major planets
SolarSystem.setFlagPlanetsOrbitsOnly(true);

name: getFlagOrbitsWithMoons
description: Returns whether moon orbits are displayed alongside planet orbits.

name: setFlagOrbitsWithMoons
description: Sets whether moon orbits should be displayed when planet orbits are shown.

name: getFlagEphemerisMarkers
description: Returns whether ephemeris markers are displayed. Ephemeris markers show predicted positions of planets at future dates.

name: setFlagEphemerisMarkers
description: Shows or hides ephemeris markers (predicted future positions of planets).

name: getFlagEphemerisLine
description: Returns whether a line connecting ephemeris markers is displayed.

name: setFlagEphemerisLine
description: Shows or hides the line connecting ephemeris markers.
example: SolarSystem.setFlagEphemerisLine(true);

name: getFlagEphemerisDates
description: Returns whether date labels are shown next to ephemeris markers.

name: setFlagEphemerisDates
description: Shows or hides date labels next to ephemeris markers.
example: SolarSystem.setFlagEphemerisDates(true);

name: getFlagEphemerisMagnitudes
description: Returns whether magnitude values are shown next to ephemeris markers.

name: setFlagEphemerisMagnitudes
description: Shows or hides magnitude values next to ephemeris markers.
example: SolarSystem.setFlagEphemerisMagnitudes(true);

name: getFlagEphemerisAlwaysOn
description: Returns whether ephemeris lines and markers are always displayed, even when planets are hidden.

name: setFlagEphemerisAlwaysOn
description: Sets whether ephemeris markers and lines are always displayed, regardless of planet visibility.
example: SolarSystem.setFlagEphemerisAlwaysOn(true);

name: getFlagEphemerisNow
description: Returns whether a special marker is shown at the current date position on the ephemeris path.

name: setFlagEphemerisNow
description: Shows or hides a special marker at the current date position on the ephemeris path.
example: SolarSystem.setFlagEphemerisNow(true);

name: getFlagEphemerisHorizontalCoordinates
description: Returns whether ephemeris markers are shown in horizontal coordinates (Alt/Az) instead of equatorial coordinates.

name: setFlagEphemerisHorizontalCoordinates
description: Sets whether ephemeris markers are shown in horizontal coordinates (Alt/Az) or equatorial coordinates.
example:
// Show ephemeris in altitude/azimuth
SolarSystem.setFlagEphemerisHorizontalCoordinates(true);

name: getEphemerisLineThickness
description: Returns the thickness of the ephemeris line in pixels.
example:
var thickness = SolarSystem.getEphemerisLineThickness();
core.debug("Ephemeris line thickness: " + thickness);

name: setEphemerisLineThickness
description: Sets the thickness of the ephemeris line in pixels.
example: SolarSystem.setEphemerisLineThickness(2);

name: getEphemerisDataStep
description: Returns the step size for skipping ephemeris markers. Higher values show fewer markers.
returnDescription: Data step (1 = show all, 2 = show every other, etc.).
example:
var step = SolarSystem.getEphemerisDataStep();
core.debug("Ephemeris step: " + step);

name: setEphemerisDataStep
description: Sets the step size for skipping ephemeris markers.

parameters.name: step
parameters.description: Step size (e.g., 5 to show every 5th marker).

example:
// Show every 5th ephemeris marker
SolarSystem.setEphemerisDataStep(5);

name: getFlagEphemerisSmartDates
description: Returns whether smart date formatting is used for ephemeris labels (showing only changed components).

name: setFlagEphemerisSmartDates
description: Enables or disables smart date formatting for ephemeris labels.
example: SolarSystem.setFlagEphemerisSmartDates(true);

name: getFlagEphemerisFirstOfMonthOnly
description: Returns whether ephemeris labels are shown only for the first day of each month.
returnDescription: `true` if only first-of-month labels are shown.

name: setFlagEphemerisFirstOfMonthOnly
description: Sets whether ephemeris labels should be shown only for the first day of each month.
example: SolarSystem.setFlagEphemerisFirstOfMonthOnly(true);

name: getLabelsColor
description: Returns the current color of planet labels.
returnDescription: RGB color vector. Use `core.getStelProperty()` to read it.

name: setLabelsColor
description: Sets the color of planet labels.

parameters.name: color
parameters.description: RGB color vector with values between 0.0 and 1.0.

name: getPointerColor
description: Returns the color of the selection pointer (animated ring around selected planets).
returnDescription: RGB color vector. Use `core.getStelProperty()` to read it.

name: setPointerColor
description: Sets the color of the selection pointer.
example: SolarSystem.setPointerColor(core.vec3f(1.0, 0.3, 0.3));

name: getTrailsColor
description: Returns the color used for planet trails.
returnDescription: RGB color vector. Use `core.getStelProperty()` to read it.

name: setTrailsColor
description: Sets the color used for planet trails.
example: SolarSystem.setTrailsColor(core.vec3f(0.5, 0.5, 0.5));

name: getOrbitsColor
description: Returns the default color of solar system object orbit lines.
returnDescription: RGB color vector. Use `core.getStelProperty()` to read it.
example: var orbitColorStr = core.getStelProperty('SolarSystem.orbitsColor');

name: setOrbitsColor
description: Sets the default color of solar system object orbit lines.

name: setMajorPlanetsOrbitsColor
description: Sets the color used for orbits of the major planets.
example: SolarSystem.setMajorPlanetsOrbitsColor(core.vec3f(0.7, 0.2, 0.2));

name: setCometsOrbitsColor
description: Sets the color used for comet orbit lines.
example: SolarSystem.setCometsOrbitsColor(core.vec3f(0.7, 0.8, 0.8));

name: getObjectList
description: Returns a list of object names filtered by type.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public` method, but should work safely.

name: getSun
description: Returns the Planet object for the Sun.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public` method, but is safe to use.

name: getEarth
description: Returns the Planet object for Earth.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public` method, but is safe to use.

example: var earth = SolarSystem.getEarth();

name: getMoon
description: Returns the Planet object for Earth's Moon.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public` method, but is safe to use.

example: var moon = SolarSystem.getMoon();

name: getPlanetVMagnitude
description: Returns the visual magnitude of a planet or solar system body. Optionally includes atmospheric extinction.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public` method, but is safe to use.

example:
var marsMag = SolarSystem.getPlanetVMagnitude("Mars");
core.debug("Mars magnitude: " + marsMag.toFixed(2));

name: getPlanetType
description: Returns the type of a solar system body (e.g., 'planet', 'asteroid', 'comet').

compatibility.notes: This is a `public` method, but is safe to use.

example:
var type = SolarSystem.getPlanetType("Ceres");
core.debug("Ceres type: " + type);

name: getDistanceToPlanet
description: Returns the distance from the observer to a planet in Astronomical Units (AU).

compatibility.notes: This is a `public` method, but is safe to use.

example:
var marsDist = SolarSystem.getDistanceToPlanet("Mars");
core.debug("Mars distance: " + marsDist.toFixed(2) + " AU");

name: getPhaseForPlanet
description: Returns the illuminated fraction (phase) of a planet or the Moon (0.0 = new, 1.0 = full).

compatibility.notes: This is a `public` method, but is safe to use.

example:
var moonPhase = SolarSystem.getPhaseForPlanet("Moon");
core.debug("Moon phase: " + (moonPhase * 100).toFixed(1) + "%");

name: removeMinorPlanet
description: Removes a minor planet (asteroid, comet, etc.) from the solar system simulation.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: English name of the minor planet to remove.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public` method, but is safe to use.

name: getExtraThreads
description: Returns the number of additional threads used for computing planet positions (beyond the main thread).
example:
var threads = SolarSystem.getExtraThreads();
core.debug("Additional computation threads: " + threads);

name: setExtraThreads
description: Sets the number of additional threads for computing planet positions. Higher values may improve performance on multi-core systems.

parameters.name: threads
parameters.description: Number of extra threads (0 to max).

example:
// Use 3 additional threads for planet calculations
SolarSystem.setExtraThreads(3);

name: getMarkerMagThreshold
description: Returns the limiting absolute magnitude for plotting minor body markers.
example:
var threshold = SolarSystem.getMarkerMagThreshold();
core.debug("Marker magnitude threshold: " + threshold);

name: setMarkerMagThreshold
description: Sets the limiting absolute magnitude for plotting minor body markers.
example:
// Show only brighter minor bodies
SolarSystem.setMarkerMagThreshold(12.0);

name: getOrbitColorStyle
description: Returns the style used for orbit line colors.
example:
var style = SolarSystem.getOrbitColorStyle();
core.debug("Orbit color style: " + style);

name: setOrbitColorStyle
description: Sets the style for orbit line colors.
example:
// Color orbits by object groups
SolarSystem.setOrbitColorStyle("groups");

name: getApparentMagnitudeAlgorithmOnEarth
description: Returns the algorithm used for computing apparent magnitudes of planets when observed from Earth.
example:
var algorithm = SolarSystem.getApparentMagnitudeAlgorithmOnEarth();
core.debug("Magnitude algorithm: " + algorithm);

name: setApparentMagnitudeAlgorithmOnEarth
description: Sets the algorithm for computing apparent magnitudes of planets.
example:
// Use modern Mallama 2018 algorithm
SolarSystem.setApparentMagnitudeAlgorithmOnEarth("Mallama2018");
